design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate multi-act platforming levels by drilling through enemies and obstacles, collecting hidden letters (A-E-R-O) to unlock bonus cup-switching mini-game for extra lives
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Exploration-driven platforming with significant hidden areas requiring player curiosity and skill to discover secrets within increasingly complex level designs
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Acrobatic bat using drill jump ability to scale a dark, gothic castle environment filled with secrets and hazards
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Sequel deliberately expands level scope and secret design philosophy from original; emphasizes exploration and environmental discovery over time pressure (no timer); integrates collectible-based progression unlock mechanic; drop-drill addition broadens tactical approach options

difficulty.lives_system: Extra lives earned through hidden letter collection cup-switching game or found as power-ups
difficulty.checkpointing: Act-based checkpoint structure; eight worlds with three acts each (except final world)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden letter collection provides optional challenge for bonus lives; secret areas add optional difficulty spikes; regional PAL version contains unbeatable level due to framerate/cannon oversight, forcing level select use

progression.upgrade_system: Drill jump ability enhancement (new downward aiming added for direct enemy attacks below); power-ups collected within levels
progression.unlock_mechanism: Collecting four hidden letters per act (spelling A-E-R-O) unlocks cup-switching bonus game at end of act; hidden level select accessible via sound test menu
replayability_hooks: Four hidden letters per act encourage replay to find all secret areas
Cup-switching bonus mini-game for extra lives
Numerous secret areas with non-linear routing options

world_building_techniques: Direct sequel narrative: follows directly after original where Aero knocked Edgar Ektor off museum tower
Environmental setting: ancient castle setting accessed via magician's box discovery
Character continuity: Zero the Kamikaze Squirrel reintroduced as henchman executing 'Plan B'
Dark gothic aesthetic reflecting castle/horror museum theme

faction_indicators: Edgar Ektor (antagonist, Museum of Horrors owner)
danger_signals: Castle setting with enemies and hazards
Gothic dark theme emphasized in design
Ektor's 'Plan B' setup suggests escalating threat
environmental_storytelling: Museum of Horrors architecture and design
Ancient castle environment suggesting antiquity and danger
Hidden areas implying mysterious castle history

sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (Aero the Acro-Bat 2 SNES page)
